results.title: The 6 Best 3D Printers of 2026, Tested by Popular Mechanics
results.content:
The Snapmaker U1 set crowdfunding records when it launched on Kickstarter, thanks to its clever multi-head system that keeps four nozzles preheated and loaded with filament. It can change tool heads on the fly, rather than changing filament fed through a single nozzle. With no need to purge older filament, the U1’s design cuts print time and dramatically reduces waste. Seeing this printer go head-to-head with a filament changer, the choice is clear: I'll take the Snapmaker every time.

The U1’s setup kit is top-notch. It has an extensive, step-by-step assembly document, with full color images of each step, and a QR code that links to a video walkthrough. I loved that it shipped with extra parts, so a broken part doesn’t bring you to a complete standstill until you order a replacement. [...] I was pleasantly surprised to see that the U1 comes with a generous amount of starter filament. It ships with four colors, each with 500 grams of filament, compared to most printers which ship with only a few grams, just enough to print a test Benchy. Snapmaker filament rolls also have RFID, so that you don’t need to manually enter the filament details. [...] 5

Best Multi-Color

## Snapmaker U1 3D Printer

Now 15% Off

~~$1,000~~ $849us.snapmaker.com

### Pros

 ProFast multi-color prints with very little wasted filament.
 ProImpeccable documentation, and robust tech support
 ProGenerous starter filaments

### Cons

 ConSlicer software is sluggish
 ConEnclosure lid requires a separate purchase

results.title: The heart of the internet
results.content:
Neither is perfect. It all depends what you want to print which is the more sensible one.

 Reply  Share 

Image 10: u/yahbluez avatar

yahbluez

•20d ago

> Another downside to toolheads is the enclosure they require and chamber hearing.

The Snapmaker U1 is a 4x tool changer without enclosure.

The enclusure can added but there is not need for if one only prints PLA.

Same the Prusa XL.

Having an enclosure is always nice but not essential for tool changers at all.

The combo of having an AMS behind every toolhead is very interesting and im sure we will see that a lot in the future.

There is the canvas just 55$ and 4 steppers for 4 filaments.

The elegoo centauri runs klipper and so i guess we soon will see klipper addons to use the canvas with every klipper based printer. [...] Reply  Share 

[](

roiki11

•20d ago

Yea I wrote incorrectly. Was meant to say if you require enclosure for certain materials that need a heated environment. Not that the printers require one.

 Reply  Share 

 more reply

more replyMore repliesMore replies

Image 11: u/yahbluez avatar

yahbluez

•20d ago

If i need to buy today it would be the U1.

If there is some time look what 6x tool changer Sovol will build.

The INDX of course if you already have a C1 or CL.

The H2C if bambufan with a lot of money.

The XL2T or XL5T if you need the size or fancy stuff to print like silikon.

The X2D is an IQ Test buyers fail the test.

Since Sovol did the step to clone Vorons they build great printers like the ZERO and the MAX. [...] Reply  Share 

 and 7 with zero purge beats my other printers. Even my U1 that's faster with 4 colors loses most of the time because it can't do more than 4 colors even though it's a faster 4 color printer. That would apply to the XL as well. Then add in the high temp material limitations of both out of the box. But that's just me it would be a waste for someone who only needs a second nozzle for support material and only does engineering materials. the X2D or another dual nozzle printer may be a better fit for them. And the U1 can be upgraded to support high temp materials and there are even AMS solutions. I'm only talking about out of the box features and print quality.

results.title: Second Century Ventures Announces 6 Technology Companies for 2026 REACH Scale-Up Program
results.content:
Ai.realestate (AiRE): Unifies unstructured internal data and pairs with property, mortgage and client life intel to provide a supercharged, living database that drives revenue for sales teams.
 Association Online (AO): Delivers a comprehensive HOA data and transparency solution designed to reduce closing delays, mitigate risk, and provide specialized support that empowers agents to navigate HOA transactions confidently and drive client loyalty.
 BrokerBot: Secure, enterprise-grade AI teammates for residential real estate that handle admin, support, training, compliance, and agent guidance—integrating seamlessly with your existing tools so brokers and agents can focus on growing their business and delivering exceptional client experiences. results.title: Physical AI Is Already Here. But What Is It?
results.content:
An illustration depicts a person going through phases of different emotional states along with different weather patterns.

## Can AI help predict how you might be feeling in the future?

A yellow Spirit Airlines plane in the air against a hazy orange sky background.

## Spirit gone, airfares high. Is now a good time to fly?

# Physical AI is already here. But what is it?

© 2026 Northeastern University [...] ## Science & Technology

President Donald Trump walks in the Rose Garden at the White House.

## Why is the government considering new rules on AI safety? Experts weigh in

From the ground, SpaceX's Starship rocket 38 launches during the 11th test flight on October 13, 2025 as seen from South Padre Island in Texas. The background is a blue sky with no clouds, as the rocket leaves behind contrails.

## A SpaceX rocket will soon hit the moon. Should you be worried?

A computer screen displaying two AI-generated goblin images in the ChatGPT interface.

## ChatGPT has a goblin problem. It’s bigger than an AI quirk.

## Recent Stories

A nurse dressed in PPE stands next to a person washing their hands while wearing a mask.

## Ebola can be contained, but the effort is just “ramping up” [...] China’s dancing robot seemed harmless enough. But in other contexts, such systems could

“How can we make sure that this action is safe, trustworthy, verifiable and something that is robust?” Ostadabbas said. “That is the final pillar in our framework.”

Wang thinks it may soon be implemented at scale, provided innovation and development continue at its current pace.

“I think it will become more mainstream, but it is still a far way to go,” Wang said. “But … based on the current progress of this generation of tools, maybe after two or three years there will be a big breakthrough.”

Tanner Stening is an assistant news editor at Northeastern Global News. Email him at t.stening@northeastern.edu. Follow him on X/Twitter @tstening90.

## Science & Technology
